finding: One review question mixed causal comparative effects with absolute rare-event incidence.
correction: Split Product A randomized comparative effects from Product B monitored-exposure safety inventory.

finding: Investigator-attributed drug-related SAE was treated as the leading serious outcome.
correction: Make all-cause SAE primary and attribution-based SAE secondary.

finding: Blank, unreported, and confirmed zero could be conflated.
correction: Add reporting-status and zero-confirmation fields; blank never means zero.

finding: Severity and regulatory seriousness were not explicitly separated.
correction: Define and extract seriousness and severity independently.

finding: Per administration and per participant could diverge in repeated-dose studies.
correction: Use dosed participants with >=1 event for direct single-dose risk; repeated regimens separate.

finding: Event-collection intensity was not a structured compatibility variable.
correction: Extract solicitation, coding, assessor, schedule, and source; stratify by ascertainment.

finding: A depression-focused search alone could miss safety reports and mixed-population records.
correction: Add a broad psilocybin-only safety-capture search without a harms filter.

finding: Open-label monitored cohorts were excluded even from descriptive rare-harm surveillance.
correction: Include prospective monitored cohorts only in a separate descriptive inventory.

finding: RoB 2 could be misapplied to nonrandomized descriptive incidence.
correction: Reserve RoB 2 for randomized comparative results; require explicit design-limitations appraisal for Product B.

finding: Study-level RoB 2 did not address missing outcomes or missing studies at synthesis level.
correction: Add ROB-ME-compatible missing-evidence assessment.

finding: The package sometimes called decision concealment “blinding.”
correction: Use decision concealment and acknowledge reviewers may know public trial results.

finding: Model choice was not explicitly locked by an independent statistician before unmasking.
correction: Add a statistical analysis plan shell and hard sign-off gate.

finding: Internal validity and applicability to other diagnoses/settings could blur.
correction: Keep population, support model, supply, and regimen as explicit transportability dimensions.

finding: Outcome selection did not formally include participant/public contributors.
correction: Add a participant/public content-review stage before registration.

finding: Substantial AI assistance was not disclosed in the scientific governance package.
correction: Add an AI assistance and human oversight statement and activity log requirement.

finding: Niacin, inactive placebo, minimal-dose psilocybin, and active treatment could be treated as interchangeable.
correction: Create comparator classes and prohibit automatic pooling.

finding: Onset, resolution, and ascertainment times could be confused.
correction: Extract each separately and prevent double-counting across 24h and 7d windows.

finding: Many harm outcomes could invite selective emphasis after results are seen.
correction: Freeze primary/secondary hierarchy and require SAP multiplicity language.

finding: The existing linkage tracked one seed record per family but not future companion reports.
correction: Retain family-first linkage and require report-level provenance during the registered search.

finding: The 13-record horizon corpus was built for randomized depression studies and is not complete for Product B.
correction: Do not expand it ad hoc; rebuild the corpus after registered focused and broad searches.
